**Position Detection**

Position detection is the process of identifying the robot's position relative to the line. This is typically achieved using sensors such as infrared (IR) sensors, photo cells, or ultrasonic sensors. IR sensors are commonly used in line following robots as they can detect the difference in reflectivity between the line and the surrounding area [[4](https://maker.pro/arduino/projects/make-line-follower-robot/)]. The IR sensor array is created by connecting multiple IR LEDs and photodiodes in a straight line, with each pair wrapped together using black insulation tape [[4](https://maker.pro/arduino/projects/make-line-follower-robot/)].

We’ve all likely used the term ‘creepy’ to describe someone or something in our lives at least once. But what constitutes creepy? Is it an underlying intuition? A safe preservation mechanism? Well, Doyle and colleagues (2022) investigated that by employing eye-tracking software to identify precisely where participants were looking the longest when examining thirty faces for attractiveness, trustworthiness, and creepiness.

They found that the eye region was by far the most looked at and that participants who had previously scored high on ambiguity discomfort were likelier to rate others as ‘creepy’ compared to those who scored highly on fear of the unknown or uncertainty intolerance (yes, these concepts ARE different).

The researchers note that while there is no evidence to support the notion that individuals labelled as ‘creepy’ genuinely pose a threat, this label may cause that individual significant harm.
